title: The Last Switchboard
summary: A young engineer and an old operator talk about the town's last switchboard.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Hello, Mrs. Olsen. I am the new engineer. Tomorrow we close the old switchboard. The town will use the new system.

body.speaker: Mrs. Olsen
body.text: Yes, I know. I have worked here for fifty years. I connect every call by hand. I move the small wires from hole to hole.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: The new system is much faster. A computer connects the calls. No one needs to wait. It is better for everyone.

body.speaker: Mrs. Olsen
body.text: You are right. It is faster. I will not say it is bad. But let me tell you one small thing first.

body.speaker: Mrs. Olsen
body.text: When old Mr. Berg is sick, he calls me. I know his voice. I talk to him for a minute. Then I connect him to the doctor.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: I see. So you are not only a machine. You know the people. The computer does not know them.

body.speaker: Mrs. Olsen
body.text: Yes. The computer is faster, but it cannot listen. Speed is good. But sometimes a slow call saves a lonely person.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: That is true. Maybe the town loses something tomorrow. Not the wires. Something warmer than wires.

body.speaker: Mrs. Olsen
body.text: Now you understand. The new system is better. And the old way had a heart. Both things are true.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for life experience
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'have/has worked ... for' để nói khoảng thời gian làm việc kéo dài đến hiện tại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Present perfect shows a span up to now: 'I have worked here for fifty years.'

grammarNotes.title: Future with 'will'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will' để nói về tương lai. Ví dụ: 'The town will use the new system.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use 'will' for the future: 'The town will use the new system.'

grammarNotes.title: Comparative adjectives
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ so sánh hơn thường thêm '-er' hoặc dùng 'more', như 'faster' và 'warmer'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Comparatives compare two things: 'The new system is much faster' and 'Something warmer than wires.'

translationVi:
Liam: Chào bà Olsen. Cháu là kỹ sư mới. Ngày mai chúng ta đóng cửa tổng đài cũ. Thị trấn sẽ dùng hệ thống mới.
Mrs. Olsen: Ừ, bà biết. Bà đã làm ở đây năm mươi năm. Bà nối từng cuộc gọi bằng tay. Bà di chuyển những sợi dây nhỏ từ lỗ này sang lỗ khác.
Liam: Hệ thống mới nhanh hơn nhiều. Một chiếc máy tính nối các cuộc gọi. Không ai phải chờ. Nó tốt hơn cho mọi người.
Mrs. Olsen: Cháu nói đúng. Nó nhanh hơn. Bà không nói nó tệ. Nhưng để bà kể cho cháu một điều nhỏ trước đã.
Liam: Dạ vâng. Bà kể đi ạ.
Mrs. Olsen: Khi ông Berg già bị ốm, ông gọi cho bà. Bà biết giọng của ông. Bà nói chuyện với ông một phút. Rồi bà nối ông với bác sĩ.
Liam: Cháu hiểu rồi. Vậy bà không chỉ là một cái máy. Bà biết những con người. Máy tính thì không biết họ.
Mrs. Olsen: Đúng vậy. Máy tính nhanh hơn, nhưng nó không biết lắng nghe. Nhanh thì tốt. Nhưng đôi khi một cuộc gọi chậm cứu được một người cô đơn.
Liam: Đúng thật. Có lẽ ngày mai thị trấn mất đi điều gì đó. Không phải những sợi dây. Một thứ ấm áp hơn cả những sợi dây.
Mrs. Olsen: Giờ thì cháu hiểu rồi. Hệ thống mới tốt hơn. Và cách cũ thì có một trái tim. Cả hai điều đều đúng.

questions.explanation: Liam says, 'I am the new engineer.'

options: Mrs. Olsen retires to the sea

questions.prompt: How long has Mrs. Olsen worked there?
questions.explanation: 'I have worked here for fifty years.'

questions.prompt: How does Mrs. Olsen connect calls?
questions.explanation: 'I connect every call by hand. I move the small wires.'

questions.prompt: Why does Liam say the new system is better?
options: It is much faster and no one waits
questions.explanation: 'The new system is much faster... No one needs to wait.'

questions.prompt: What does Mrs. Olsen do when Mr. Berg is sick?
options: She talks to him, then connects him to the doctor
questions.explanation: 'I talk to him for a minute. Then I connect him to the doctor.'
questions.hint: Mrs. Olsen's line about Mr. Berg.

questions.prompt: What can the old way do that the computer cannot?
questions.explanation: 'The computer is faster, but it cannot listen.'
questions.hint: Mrs. Olsen says this near the end.

questions.prompt: What does Liam mean by 'Something warmer than wires'?
questions.explanation: He means the human care, not the wires themselves.

questions.prompt: Which sentence best shows both speakers agree on something?
options: 'The new system is better. And the old way had a heart. Both things are true.'
'I have worked here for fifty years.'
questions.explanation: Mrs. Olsen's last line says both things are true.

questions.prompt: In the dialogue, 'lonely' is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: 'A slow call saves a lonely person' means someone alone and sad.
questions.hint: Mrs. Olsen's line about speed and a slow call.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: Faster can be better and still lose something human
questions.explanation: Both agree the system is better but the old way had a heart.
questions.hint: Think about the whole talk, not one line.

questions.prompt: How does Liam's view change during the talk?
options: He decides the old way is useless
He starts to see what the town might lose
He wants to quit his job
He stops believing in the computer
questions.explanation: He moves from 'better for everyone' to 'Maybe the town loses something.'
questions.hint: Compare Liam's first and later lines.

blanks.explanation: She has worked there for fifty years.
She connects each call by hand.
The new system is much faster.
When Mr. Berg is sick, he calls her.
She connects him to the doctor.
A slow call saves a lonely person.
The old way had a heart.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mrs. Olsen says, 'The new system is better. And the old way had a heart. Both things are true.' When something faster replaces something slower, what kind of human thing might we lose, and is it worth saving?
